skill.name: qms-audit-expert
skill.description: Senior QMS Audit Expert for internal and external quality management system auditing. Provides ISO 13485 audit expertise, audit program management, nonconformity identification, and corrective action verification. Use for internal audit planning, external audit preparation, audit execution, and audit follow-up activities.
skill.summary: Plan, execute, and improve ISO 13485 QMS audit programs for medical device organizations.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography, shell execution, environment access, and network reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found those alerts were false positives from Markdown prose, tree diagrams, placeholder resource names, and quality-management terminology. No prompt injection, credential access, data exfiltration, executable code, or malicious intent was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Keywords
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography findings match ordinary audit and placeholder wording, such as design controls, API reference examples, and asset examples. No cryptographic function, hash implementation, encryption API, or security-sensitive algorithm use was found at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain prose, headings, or placeholder file examples. There is no code path that performs cryptography or weak hashing.

low_findings.title: False Positive: External Command Execution
low_findings.description: The external command detections occur in Markdown code fences, directory-style diagrams, and resource lists. They describe QMS audit concepts and script filenames, but they do not execute Ruby backticks, shell commands, or any other command runner.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only backtick-delimited content is Markdown formatting for diagrams and filenames. No executable language block invokes a shell or passes user input to a command.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Environment Access
low_findings.description: The environment-access detections match business-language terms such as management, classification, and forms. No environment variable API, configuration reader, secret lookup, or credential handling was found in the reviewed lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are plain-text QMS guidance and resource names. They do not reference process.env, os.environ, dotenv, or another secret source.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance alerts match medical-device audit terminology for risk management and post-market surveillance. No network scanning, host discovery, port enumeration, or remote connection behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines describe ISO 14971, IEC 62304, and post-market surveillance audit topics. They are domain content, not network tooling instructions.

content.value_statement: Medical device teams need structured audits that connect ISO 13485 requirements to real QMS evidence. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan audits, classify findings, and verify CAPA follow-up.
actual_capabilities: Build risk-based internal audit plans for ISO 13485 QMS processes.
Define audit scope, criteria, methods, and evidence collection activities.
Classify audit findings as major nonconformities, minor nonconformities, observations, or best practices.
Support external audit readiness for regulatory inspections, certification body audits, and customer audits.
Connect audit findings to CAPA initiation, corrective action verification, and follow-up audits.
Suggest audit program metrics for schedule compliance, finding quality, and CAPA effectiveness.
limitations: It does not replace a qualified regulatory auditor or legal review.
It provides guidance, not certification or regulatory approval.
It relies on user-provided QMS context and evidence.
Placeholder reference and asset files do not include detailed templates.

use_cases.title: Create an Annual Internal Audit Plan
use_cases.description: Build a risk-based schedule that covers high-risk, medium-risk, and low-risk QMS processes.

use_cases.title: Prepare for an External Audit
use_cases.description: Organize readiness checks, personnel roles, documentation review, and response planning before an inspection.

use_cases.title: Improve CAPA Follow-Up
use_cases.description: Translate audit findings into corrective action verification and follow-up audit activities.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Basic Audit Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for one QMS process.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an ISO 13485 internal audit plan for [process]. Include scope, criteria, audit methods, required records, and opening meeting topics.

prompt_templates.title: Classify Audit Findings
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of collected audit evidence.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these audit observations and classify each as a major nonconformity, minor nonconformity, observation, or best practice: [observations]. Explain the reason for each classification.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare External Audit Readiness
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced preparation for a regulator, certification body, or customer audit.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a readiness plan for a [regulatory inspection/certification audit/customer audit]. Include pre-audit checks, document review, personnel roles, logistics, and escalation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize an Audit Program
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced improvement across an annual audit program.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this audit program data: [data]. Recommend changes to audit frequency, scope, auditor competency plans, CAPA verification, and performance metrics.

output_examples.input: Plan an audit for design controls under ISO 13485.
output_examples.output: A structured audit plan with objectives, scope, clauses, records to sample, interview targets, evidence collection methods, and expected report sections.

output_examples.input: Classify these audit observations and propose follow-up actions.
output_examples.output: A finding list with severity classifications, requirement references, evidence summaries, CAPA triggers, and verification activities.

output_examples.input: Assess our annual audit program performance.
output_examples.output: A concise performance review covering schedule completion, finding trends, auditor competency gaps, CAPA effectiveness, and program improvement actions.

best_practices: Provide the relevant ISO 13485 clauses, process scope, and available QMS evidence.
Ask for finding classifications with evidence and requirement references.
Use outputs as draft audit material for review by qualified quality personnel.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to certify compliance without objective evidence.
Do not use it as the only review before a regulatory inspection.
Do not include confidential patient, employee, or proprietary data unless approved.

faq.question: Can this skill perform a full ISO 13485 certification audit?
faq.answer: No. It helps prepare audit plans, findings, and follow-up actions, but it cannot provide certification.

faq.question: Does it support medical device QMS audits?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill focuses on ISO 13485 audit planning, execution, nonconformity management, and CAPA verification.

faq.question: Can it help with external audit preparation?
faq.answer: Yes. It can structure readiness activities for regulatory inspections, certification body audits, and customer audits.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the process, audit scope, relevant clauses, previous findings, QMS records, and any known risks.

faq.question: Can it classify nonconformities?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft major, minor, observation, and best-practice classifications based on supplied evidence.

faq.question: Does it include executable automation?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill content references possible resources, but the available files are guidance and placeholders.
